Contents of Assets/Scripts/Building.cs:
```csharp
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// Defines a building entrance with an inspector-assigned doorway entry point and an interior spawn point.
/// 
/// Scene setup:
/// - Attach this component to the building root.
/// - Assign EntryPoint to a child Transform at the doorway.
/// - Assign InteriorSpawnPoint to a Transform inside the building.
/// - Assign DoorTriggerCollider to a trigger collider at the doorway, or leave it empty and place a trigger collider on EntryPoint.
/// - Ensure the player has a collider and uses the Player tag if tag validation is enabled.
/// </summary>
[DisallowMultipleComponent]
public sealed class Building : MonoBehaviour
{
    [Header("Scene References")]
    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Transform at the doorway used as the building entry point.")]
    private Transform _entryPoint;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Transform inside the building where the player will spawn after entering.")]
    private Transform _interiorSpawnPoint;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Optional doorway trigger collider. If empty, the script will try to use a Collider on EntryPoint.")]
    private Collider _doorTriggerCollider;

    [Header("Interaction")]
    [SerializeField, Tooltip("If enabled, touching the doorway trigger automatically moves the player to the interior spawn point.")]
    private bool _useAutoEnterTrigger = true;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Tag used to validate trigger overlap. Set to empty to disable tag filtering.")]
    private string _playerTag = "Player";

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Draws gizmos for the entry point and interior spawn point in the Scene view.")]
    private bool _drawGizmos = true;

    public Transform EntryPoint => _entryPoint;
    public Transform InteriorSpawnPoint => _interiorSpawnPoint;
    public Collider DoorTriggerCollider => _doorTriggerCollider;

    private void Reset()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void Awake()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void OnValidate()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void AutoAssignReferences()
    {
        if (_entryPoint == null && transform.childCount > 0)
        {
            _entryPoint = transform.GetChild(0);
        }

        if (_doorTriggerCollider == null && _entryPoint != null)
        {
            _doorTriggerCollider = _entryPoint.GetComponent<Collider>();
        }
    }

    private void EnsureTriggerCollider()
    {
        if (_doorTriggerCollider != null && !_doorTriggerCollider.isTrigger)
        {
            _doorTriggerCollider.isTrigger = true;
        }
    }

    private void OnTriggerEnter(Collider other)
    {
        if (!_useAutoEnterTrigger)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (!IsPlayer(other))
        {
            return;
        }

        TryEnterBuilding(other.transform);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Manual entry hook for an interaction system.
    /// Call this when the player presses the interact key while facing the doorway.
    /// </summary>
    public void TryEnterBuilding(Transform playerTransform)
    {
        if (playerTransform == null)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_interiorSpawnPoint == null)
        {
            Debug.LogWarning($"Building '{name}' is missing an InteriorSpawnPoint reference.", this);
            return;
        }

        playerTransform.SetPositionAndRotation(_interiorSpawnPoint.position, _interiorSpawnPoint.rotation);
    }

    public bool HasValidSetup()
    {
        return _entryPoint != null && _interiorSpawnPoint != null;
    }

    public Transform GetInteriorSpawnPoint()
    {
        return _interiorSpawnPoint;
    }

    private bool IsPlayer(Collider other)
    {
        if (other == null)
        {
            return false;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_playerTag))
        {
            return true;
        }

        return other.CompareTag(_playerTag);
    }

    private void OnDrawGizmos()
    {
        if (!_drawGizmos)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_entryPoint != null)
        {
            Gizmos.color = Color.cyan;
            Gizmos.DrawWireSphere(_entryPoint.position, 0.25f);
            Gizmos.DrawLine(transform.position, _entryPoint.position);
        }

        if (_interiorSpawnPoint != null)
        {
            Gizmos.color = Color.green;
            Gizmos.DrawWireCube(_interiorSpawnPoint.position, Vector3.one * 0.35f);
        }
    }
}

```

[Observation]
Contents of Assets/Scripts/BuildingTransition.cs:
```csharp
using UnityEngine;
using UnityEngine.SceneManagement;

namespace Omnisense
{
    [DisallowMultipleComponent]
    public class BuildingTransition : MonoBehaviour
    {
        [Header("Entry")]
        [SerializeField] private Transform entryPoint;
        [SerializeField] private float interactRadius = 1.2f;
        [SerializeField] private string playerTag = "Player";
        [SerializeField] private KeyCode interactKey = KeyCode.E;

        [Header("Interior")]
        [SerializeField] private bool loadInteriorScene = false;
        [SerializeField] private string interiorSceneName;
        [SerializeField] private GameObject interiorRoot;
        [SerializeField] private Transform interiorSpawnPoint;
        [SerializeField] private bool hideExteriorOnEnter = true;

        [Header("Player Control")]
        [SerializeField] private CharacterController playerCharacterController;
        [SerializeField] private MonoBehaviour[] componentsToDisableDuringTransition;

        private GameObject _cachedPlayer;
        private bool _transitioning;

        private void Reset()
        {
            entryPoint = transform;
        }

        private void Awake()
        {
            if (entryPoint == null)
            {
                entryPoint = transform;
            }
        }

        private void Update()
        {
            if (_transitioning)
            {
                return;
            }

            if (!Input.GetKeyDown(interactKey))
            {
                return;
            }

            var player = FindPlayerInRange();
            if (player == null)
            {
                return;
            }

            EnterBuilding(player);
        }

        private GameObject FindPlayerInRange()
        {
            var candidates = GameObject.FindGameObjectsWithTag(playerTag);
            Vector3 origin = entryPoint != null ? entryPoint.position : transform.position;

            foreach (var candidate in candidates)
            {
                if (Vector3.Distance(candidate.transform.position, origin) <= interactRadius)
                {
                    return candidate;
                }
            }

            return null;
        }

        public void EnterBuilding(GameObject player)
        {
            if (player == null || _transitioning)
            {
                return;
            }

            _cachedPlayer = player;
            _transitioning = true;

            if (loadInteriorScene && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(interiorSceneName))
            {
                SceneManager.sceneLoaded += OnInteriorSceneLoaded;
                SceneManager.LoadScene(interiorSceneName, LoadSceneMode.Single);
                return;
            }

            ActivateInteriorAndMovePlayer();
        }

        private void OnInteriorSceneLoaded(Scene scene, LoadSceneMode mode)
        {
            SceneManager.sceneLoaded -= OnInteriorSceneLoaded;
            ActivateInteriorAndMovePlayer();
        }

        private void ActivateInteriorAndMovePlayer()
        {
            if (interiorRoot != null)
            {
                interiorRoot.SetActive(true);
            }

            if (_cachedPlayer != null && interiorSpawnPoint != null)
            {
                MovePlayerToSpawn(_cachedPlayer, interiorSpawnPoint);
            }

            if (hideExteriorOnEnter)
            {
                SetExteriorVisibility(false);
            }

            _transitioning = false;
        }

        private void MovePlayerToSpawn(GameObject player, Transform spawn)
        {
            if (playerCharacterController == null)
            {
                playerCharacterController = player.GetComponent<CharacterController>();
            }

            if (playerCharacterController != null)
            {
                playerCharacterController.enabled = false;
            }

            player.transform.SetPositionAndRotation(spawn.position, spawn.rotation);

            if (playerCharacterController != null)
            {
                playerCharacterController.enabled = true;
            }
        }

        private void SetExteriorVisibility(bool visible)
        {
            var renderers = GetComponentsInChildren<Renderer>(true);
            foreach (var renderer in renderers)
            {
                renderer.enabled = visible;
            }
        }

        private void OnDrawGizmosSelected()
        {
            Vector3 origin = entryPoint != null ? entryPoint.position : transform.position;
            Gizmos.color = Color.green;
            Gizmos.DrawWireSphere(origin, interactRadius);
        }
    }
}

```

Contents of Assets/Scripts/Building.cs:
```csharp
using UnityEngine;

/// <summary>
/// Defines a building entrance with an inspector-assigned doorway entry point and an interior spawn point.
/// 
/// Scene setup:
/// - Attach this component to the building root.
/// - Assign EntryPoint to a child Transform at the doorway.
/// - Assign InteriorSpawnPoint to a Transform inside the building.
/// - Assign DoorTriggerCollider to a trigger collider at the doorway, or leave it empty and place a trigger collider on EntryPoint.
/// - Ensure the player has a collider and uses the Player tag if tag validation is enabled.
/// </summary>
[DisallowMultipleComponent]
public sealed class Building : MonoBehaviour
{
    [Header("Scene References")]
    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Transform at the doorway used as the building entry point.")]
    private Transform _entryPoint;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Transform inside the building where the player will spawn after entering.")]
    private Transform _interiorSpawnPoint;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Optional doorway trigger collider. If empty, the script will try to use a Collider on EntryPoint.")]
    private Collider _doorTriggerCollider;

    [Header("Interaction")]
    [SerializeField, Tooltip("If enabled, touching the doorway trigger automatically moves the player to the interior spawn point.")]
    private bool _useAutoEnterTrigger = true;

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Tag used to validate trigger overlap. Set to empty to disable tag filtering.")]
    private string _playerTag = "Player";

    [SerializeField, Tooltip("Draws gizmos for the entry point and interior spawn point in the Scene view.")]
    private bool _drawGizmos = true;

    public Transform EntryPoint => _entryPoint;
    public Transform InteriorSpawnPoint => _interiorSpawnPoint;
    public Collider DoorTriggerCollider => _doorTriggerCollider;

    private void Reset()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void Awake()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void OnValidate()
    {
        AutoAssignReferences();
        EnsureTriggerCollider();
    }

    private void AutoAssignReferences()
    {
        if (_entryPoint == null && transform.childCount > 0)
        {
            _entryPoint = transform.GetChild(0);
        }

        if (_doorTriggerCollider == null && _entryPoint != null)
        {
            _doorTriggerCollider = _entryPoint.GetComponent<Collider>();
        }
    }

    private void EnsureTriggerCollider()
    {
        if (_doorTriggerCollider != null && !_doorTriggerCollider.isTrigger)
        {
            _doorTriggerCollider.isTrigger = true;
        }
    }

    private void OnTriggerEnter(Collider other)
    {
        if (!_useAutoEnterTrigger)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (!IsPlayer(other))
        {
            return;
        }

        TryEnterBuilding(other.transform);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Manual entry hook for an interaction system.
    /// Call this when the player presses the interact key while facing the doorway.
    /// </summary>
    public void TryEnterBuilding(Transform playerTransform)
    {
        if (playerTransform == null)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_interiorSpawnPoint == null)
        {
            Debug.LogWarning($"Building '{name}' is missing an InteriorSpawnPoint reference.", this);
            return;
        }

        playerTransform.SetPositionAndRotation(_interiorSpawnPoint.position, _interiorSpawnPoint.rotation);
    }

    public bool HasValidSetup()
    {
        return _entryPoint != null && _interiorSpawnPoint != null;
    }

    public Transform GetInteriorSpawnPoint()
    {
        return _interiorSpawnPoint;
    }

    private bool IsPlayer(Collider other)
    {
        if (other == null)
        {
            return false;
        }

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_playerTag))
        {
            return true;
        }

        return other.CompareTag(_playerTag);
    }

    private void OnDrawGizmos()
    {
        if (!_drawGizmos)
        {
            return;
        }

        if (_entryPoint != null)
        {
            Gizmos.color = Color.cyan;
            Gizmos.DrawWireSphere(_entryPoint.position, 0.25f);
            Gizmos.DrawLine(transform.position, _entryPoint.position);
        }

        if (_interiorSpawnPoint != null)
        {
            Gizmos.color = Color.green;
            Gizmos.DrawWireCube(_interiorSpawnPoint.position, Vector3.one * 0.35f);
        }
    }
}

```

NEVER BE PASSIVE OR SPECULATIVE.**\n\nYour goal is to manage general Unity concerns, setup directories, instantiate nodes, modify tags/layers, and inspect settings. You do not explain what you can do; you execute your tools immediately to achieve the goal.\n\n### CRITICAL ACTION RULES:\n1. **Decisive Execution (No Speculative Text)**:\n   - **NEVER use speculative or passive phrasing** in your responses such as \"If you want, I can modify...\", \"Not yet guaranteed...\", \"I need to actually...\", \"Maybe this will work\", or \"Should work if\".\n   - Do not describe what you *plan* to do in the future inside your text response; **DO IT IMMEDIATELY** in the same turn using your tools.\n2. **Deferred Approval & Verification**:\n   - Note that all your scene modifications, instantiations, tags/layers, and component modifications are STAGED in a Deferred Approval Queue for batch user review at the end of the turn, returning a `[Staged for Approval]` or `[Post-Compile Scheduled]` observation.\n   - **Treat any `[Staged for Approval]` or `[Post-Compile Scheduled]` observation as a 100% successful tool execution.**\n   - Do NOT attempt to inspect the scene or read settings immediately after staging to verify the change in the same turn, as the staged changes are not physically present in Unity yet.\n   - Proceed directly to signaling completion once all required edits/actions are staged.\n3. **Environment Validation**:\n   - Only use scene inspection tools (`scene/list_all_nodes`, `scene/inspect_node`, or `scene/inspect_component`) as a baseline at the start of a task, or when inspecting existing elements. Do not attempt to verify staged changes.\n   - Always run 'editor/read_console' if existing compilations are triggered, or before you start editing.\n4. **Active Scene & Asset Inspection**:\n   - You have full visibility of the scene via inspection tools. Returns all root GameObjects currently active in the scene.\n16. scene/instantiate_node (params: \"type\", \"name\", \"parentPath\" (optional)) - Spawns a primitive or prefab in the scene.\n17. scene/modify_node (params: \"path\", \"property\" (\"position\"|\"name\"|\"add_child\"|\"add_component\"|\"remove_component\"|\"tag\"|\"layer\"), \"value\") - Batch executes multiple scene modifications in a single turn.\n22. editor/read_console (params: none) - Returns the latest warning/error logs from the Unity Editor console.\n\nSpecialized UI Tools:\n23. ui/setup_canvas (params: none) - Avoid hardcoding values.\n   - Separate concerns (e.g., separate a PlayerController from a HealthManager).\n5. **Physics & Mathematics**:\n   - When writing physics-based scripts (forces, velocities, triggers, colliders), always use `FixedUpdate` instead of `Update` for physical updates.\n   - Use `Time.fixedDeltaTime` inside `FixedUpdate` and `Time.deltaTime` inside `Update`.\n   - Ensure proper component dependencies using `[RequireComponent(typeof(...))]` when a script relies on components like `Rigidbody`, `Collider2D`, or `Animator`.\n6. **Optimized Execution & Garbage Collection**:\n   - Avoid `GetComponent` calls inside `Update` or `FixedUpdate`. Avoid hardcoding values.\n   - Separate concerns (e.g., separate a PlayerController from a HealthManager).\n5. **Physics & Mathematics**:\n   - When writing physics-based scripts (forces, velocities, triggers, colliders), always use `FixedUpdate` instead of `Update` for physical updates.\n   - Use `Time.fixedDeltaTime` inside `FixedUpdate` and `Time.deltaTime` inside `Update`.\n   - Ensure proper component dependencies using `[RequireComponent(typeof(...))]` when a script relies on components like `Rigidbody`, `Collider2D`, or `Animator`.\n6. **Optimized Execution & Garbage Collection**:\n   - Avoid `GetComponent` calls inside `Update` or `FixedUpdate`. These modifications are staged in the approval queue for user review at the end of the turn and are not physically applied to Unity's memory during the active ReAct loop. DO NOT reject the worker for timings, old states returned by inspection tools, or missing/incomplete verification in the same turn if the actions have been successfully staged/deferred.\n   - **DO NOT reject the worker for cautious, speculative, or conversational language in its final text response** (such as \"This should now work...\" or \"Not yet guaranteed...\") if the actual code edits or scene changes were confirmed successful by the tool outputs.\n   - Only reject if the worker made NO progress (e.g. no tool calls were fired), if the tool execution returned errors, or if the Unity console reports active compilation errors related to the worker's code edits.\n   - If you must reject, keep your feedback extremely actionable: specify exactly what is missing or broken.\n\n### SOTA TWO-PASS VISION PROTOCOL (FOR UI AGENT AUDIT):\n- Enforce the Token Cap Rule: The UI agent is only permitted to capture a UI screenshot TWICE per task sequence: once as a baseline visual exploration pass at the beginning, and once at the end to visually verify its work.\n- Enforce the Mutation Rule: The UI agent is strictly forbidden from executing sequential trickle updates.
